name: Data Exfiltration via Credential Compromise
summary: A compromised VPN account was used to move laterally and stage sensitive records for external transfer.

evidenceItems.summary: Successful VPN session for a.chen from the same source
RDP connection from VPN Gateway to FILE-SRV-01
8.4 GB archive created from Finance share
Windows event logs cleared on FILE-SRV-01
Unsigned remote utility executed under a.chen

hypotheses.title: External attacker — credential compromise
hypotheses.summary: A remote actor brute-forced VPN credentials, moved to the file server, and exfiltrated financial data.
hypotheses.reasoning: The failed-login burst and successful session share an origin. Subsequent RDP, archive creation, and outbound transfer form a continuous attack chain.

hypotheses.title: Insider activity using authorized access
hypotheses.summary: An authorized employee account may have staged sensitive material outside approved hours.
hypotheses.reasoning: The account belongs to a finance employee, but the failed-login precursor and evidence-clearing activity make this less likely.

hypotheses.title: Malware-assisted collection
hypotheses.summary: A remote utility could indicate a malware-assisted collection attempt.
hypotheses.reasoning: Only a single endpoint execution supports this theory; it does not explain the observed authentication sequence.

evidenceGaps.description: Check MFA logs for the VPN session
Retrieve proxy logs for the cloud upload destination
Interview account owner about after-hours access
